children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is established an Advisory Committee on International Exchange Rate Policy (in this section referred to as the “Committee”).

children.heading: Duties
children.label: 2 Duties
children.text: The Committee shall be responsible for advising the Secretary of the Treasury with respect to the impact of international exchange rates and financial policies on the economy of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: Three members shall be appointed by the President pro tempore of the Senate, upon the recommendation of the chairmen and ranking members of the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs and the Committee on Finance of the Senate.

children.label: B
children.text: Three members shall be appointed by the Speaker of the House of Representatives, upon the recommendation of the chairmen and ranking members of the Committee on Financial Services and the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives.

children.label: C
children.text: Three members shall be appointed by the President.

children.heading: Qualifications
children.label: 2 Qualifications
children.text: Members shall be selected under paragraph (1) on the basis of their objectivity and demonstrated expertise in finance, economics, or currency exchange.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Members shall be appointed for a term of 2 years or until the Committee terminates.

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: B Reappointment
children.text: A member may be reappointed to the Committee for additional terms.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: 4 Vacancies
children.text: Any vacancy in the Committee shall not affect its powers, but shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Committee shall terminate on the date that is 2 years after , unless renewed by the President for a subsequent 2-year period.February 24, 2016

children.heading: Continued renewal
children.label: 2 Continued renewal
children.text: The President may continue to renew the Committee for successive 2-year periods by taking appropriate action to renew the Committee prior to the date on which the Committee would otherwise terminate.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: d Meetings
children.text: The Committee shall hold not fewer than 2 meetings each calendar year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Committee shall elect from among its members a chairperson for a term of 2 years or until the Committee terminates.

children.heading: Reelection; subsequent terms
children.label: 2 Reelection; subsequent terms
children.text: A chairperson of the Committee may be reelected chairperson but is ineligible to serve consecutive terms as chairperson.

children.heading: Staff
children.label: f Staff
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall make available to the Committee such staff, information, personnel, administrative services, and assistance as the Committee may reasonably require to carry out the activities of the Committee.

children.heading: Application of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: g Application of chapter 10 of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the provisions of chapter 10 of title 5 shall apply to the Committee.

children.label: A
children.text: would seriously compromise the development by the Government of the United States of monetary or financial policy; or

children.label: i
children.text: lead to significant financial speculation in currencies, securities, or commodities; or

children.label: ii
children.text: significantly endanger the stability of any financial institution.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary of the Treasury for each fiscal year in which the Committee is in effect $1,000,000 to carry out this section.
